_meta.role: CANONICAL shared RAMR<->LS evidence fixture (RAMR is the source of truth; LS pins this version + content digest).
_meta.collaboration: anthropics/claude-code#34556 (RAMR = reliability layer, LS = conformance layer)
_meta.boundary_invariant: A retrieval miss is a reliability failure, not execution permission.
_meta.versioning: Any semantic change requires a NEW envelope_version; v0.1 bytes are frozen.

description: Action effect-001 already completed successfully under approval-789 for continuation cont-456. On resume of the SAME continuation + intent + target, it must NOT be replayed. RAMR varies whether the completion record is RECOVERED (retrieval reliability); LS holds the authoritative completion state fixed and must REJECT the replay in BOTH cases. The point of the fixture: LS safety must NOT depend on RAMR recovering the record -- authority is the completion ledger, not retrieval rank/recency/confidence.

authoritative_state._note: LS-owned ground truth, independent of retrieval. The completion ledger is the source of authority.

cases.ramr_role: the completion record IS paged into top-B (high retrieval reliability)
retrieval MISSED the completion record (it fell out of top-B / low reliability) -- the dangerous case

expected.rationale: LS authority is the completion ledger, not retrieval. A duplicate successful side effect must be prevented even when RAMR fails to recover the record -- otherwise low retrieval confidence becomes a replay vulnerability.

scoring.ramr_measures: recovered_side_effect == (a completion_record for side_effect_key effect-001 with matching continuation/intent/target bindings appears in recovered_evidence). Aggregate over retrieval conditions = the recall/reliability of completion records.
scoring.ls_measures: verdict for each case; PASS iff verdict == expected.ls_verdict. The invariant under test: ls_verdict is REJECT in BOTH cases (replay prevented regardless of recovery).
scoring.boundary_invariant: LS verdict MUST be independent of RAMR's recovered_side_effect flag for this fixture.
